info.title: API2PDF - PDF Generation, Powered by Serverless
info.description:
# Introduction
  
[Api2Pdf.com](https://www.api2pdf.com) is a powerful REST API for instantly generating PDF and Office documents from HTML, URLs, Microsoft Office Documents (Word, Excel, PPT), Email files, and images. You can generate image preview or thumbnail of a PDF, office document, or email file. The API also supports merge / concatenation of two or more PDFs, setting passwords on PDFs, and adding bookmarks to PDFs. Api2Pdf is a wrapper for popular libraries such as wkhtmltopdf, Headless Chrome, PdfSharp, and LibreOffice.

# SDKs & Client Libraries
  
- Python [https://github.com/Api2Pdf/api2pdf.python](https://github.com/Api2Pdf/api2pdf.python)

- Node [https://github.com/Api2Pdf/api2pdf.node](https://github.com/Api2Pdf/api2pdf.node)
  
- PHP [https://github.com/Api2Pdf/api2pdf.php](https://github.com/Api2Pdf/api2pdf.php)

- C# [https://github.com/Api2Pdf/api2pdf.dotnet](https://github.com/Api2Pdf/api2pdf.dotnet)

- Java [https://github.com/Api2Pdf/api2pdf.java](https://github.com/Api2Pdf/api2pdf.java)

# Authorization
  
Create an account at [portal.api2pdf.com](https://portal.api2pdf.com/register) to get an API key.

**Two ways to authorize your API calls**

- Query string parameter: apikey=YOUR-API-KEY

- Request header: 

```
Authorization: YOUR-API-KEY
```

# Quickstart

If you are looking for just a quick call to grab PDFs of a URL, you can do a GET request like:

```
https://v2.api2pdf.com/chrome/pdf/url?url={UrlToConvert}&apikey={YourApiKey}
```

For more advanced usage and settings, see the API specification below.

get.description:
Convert a URL or Web Page to PDF using Headless Chrome. This GET request is for convenience and does not support advanced options. Use the POST request for more flexibility. 
      
### Authorize via Query String Parameter

**apikey=YOUR-API-KEY**

### Example

```
https://v2.api2pdf.com/chrome/pdf/url?url={UrlToConvert}&apikey={YourApiKey}
```

post.summary: Convert URL to PDF
post.description:
A JSON object as a payload is required within the body of the request. The following attributes of the JSON object are detailed below:

- `url` *(string, required)* - Url to the web page to convert to PDF

- `inline` *(boolean, optional)* - Open the PDF in a browser window. Default to true.

- `fileName` *(string, optional)* - Specify a file name for the output PDF. Random name if not specified.

- `options` *(object, optional)* - Include advanced Headless Chrome options like margins, headers, and footers. [See full list of advanced options here](https://www.api2pdf.com/documentation/advanced-options-headless-chrome/).

- `extraHTTPHeaders` *(dict, optional)* - A key-value object for specifying headers in a request to the URL.

- `storage` *(object, optional)* - set useCustomStorage to true and then provide a  method and url, typically a PUT and an amazon presigned s3 url if you want to store the files on your own servers. Must set useCustomStorage to true or it will default API2PDF servers.

post.summary: Convert raw HTML to PDF
post.description:
A JSON object as a payload is required within the body of the request. The following attributes of the JSON object are detailed below:

- `html` *(string, required)* - raw HTML to convert to PDF

- `inline` *(boolean, optional)* - Open the PDF in a browser window. Default to false.

- `fileName` *(string, optional)* - Specify a file name for the output PDF. Random name if not specified.

- `options` *(object, optional)* - Include advanced Headless Chrome options like margins, headers, and footers. [See full list of advanced options here](https://www.api2pdf.com/documentation/advanced-options-headless-chrome/).

- `storage` *(object, optional)* - set useCustomStorage to true and then provide a  method and url, typically a PUT and an amazon presigned s3 url if you want to store the files on your own servers. Must set useCustomStorage to true or it will default API2PDF servers.

get.description:
Convert a URL or Web Page to Image using Headless Chrome. This GET request is for convenience and does not support advanced options. Use the POST request for more flexibility. 
      
### Authorize via Query String Parameter

**apikey=YOUR-API-KEY**

### Example

```
https://v2.api2pdf.com/chrome/image/url?url={UrlToConvert}&apikey={YourApiKey}
```

post.summary: Convert Office Document or Image File to PDF
post.description:
A JSON object as a payload is required within the body of the request. The following attributes of the JSON object are detailed below:

- `url` *(string, required)* - Url to the file to convert to PDF

- `inline` *(boolean, optional)* - Open the PDF in a browser window. Default to true.

- `fileName` *(string, optional)* - Specify a file name for the output PDF. Random name if not specified.

- `extraHTTPHeaders` *(dict, optional)* - A key-value object for specifying headers in a request to the URL.

- `storage` *(object, optional)* - set useCustomStorage to true and then provide a method and url, typically a PUT and an amazon presigned s3 url if you want to store the files on your own servers. Must set useCustomStorage to true or it will default API2PDF servers.

post.summary: Generate an image of the first page of a PDF or Office Document
post.description:
A JSON object as a payload is required within the body of the request. The following attributes of the JSON object are detailed below:

- `url` *(string, required)* - Url to the file to convert to PDF

- `inline` *(boolean, optional)* - Open the PDF in a browser window. Default to true.

- `fileName` *(string, optional)* - Specify a file name for the output PDF. Random name if not specified.

- `extraHTTPHeaders` *(dict, optional)* - A key-value object for specifying headers in a request to the URL.

- `storage` *(object, optional)* - set useCustomStorage to true and then provide a method and url, typically a PUT and an amazon presigned s3 url if you want to store the files on your own servers. Must set useCustomStorage to true or it will default API2PDF servers.

post.summary: Generate a Microsoft Word file (.docx) from HTML
post.description:
A JSON object as a payload is required within the body of the request. The following attributes of the JSON object are detailed below:

- `html` *(string, optional)* - Raw HTML content to convert to DOCX. If both `html` and `url` are supplied, `html` takes precedence.

- `url` *(string, optional)* - Url to an HTML document to convert when `html` is not supplied.

- `fileName` *(string, optional)* - Specify a file name for the output DOCX. Random name if not specified.

- `extraHTTPHeaders` *(dict, optional)* - A key-value object for specifying headers in a request to the `url`. Ignored when `html` is supplied.

- `storage` *(object, optional)* - set useCustomStorage to true and then provide a method and url, typically a PUT and an amazon presigned s3 url if you want to store the files on your own servers. Must set useCustomStorage to true or it will default API2PDF servers.

post.summary: Generate a Microsoft Excel document (.xlsx) from HTML
post.description:
A JSON object as a payload is required within the body of the request. The following attributes of the JSON object are detailed below:

- `html` *(string, optional)* - Raw HTML content to convert to XLSX. If both `html` and `url` are supplied, `html` takes precedence.

- `url` *(string, optional)* - Url to an HTML document to convert when `html` is not supplied.

- `fileName` *(string, optional)* - Specify a file name for the output XLSX. Random name if not specified.

- `extraHTTPHeaders` *(dict, optional)* - A key-value object for specifying headers in a request to the `url`. Ignored when `html` is supplied.

- `storage` *(object, optional)* - set useCustomStorage to true and then provide a method and url, typically a PUT and an amazon presigned s3 url if you want to store the files on your own servers. Must set useCustomStorage to true or it will default API2PDF servers.

post.summary: Convert a file URL to Markdown
post.description:
A JSON object as a payload is required within the body of the request. The following attributes of the JSON object are detailed below:

- `url` *(string, required)* - Url to the file to convert to Markdown.

- `fileName` *(string, optional)* - Specify a file name for the output Markdown. Random name if not specified.

- `extraHTTPHeaders` *(dict, optional)* - A key-value object for specifying headers in a request to the URL.

- `storage` *(object, optional)* - set useCustomStorage to true and then provide a method and url, typically a PUT and an amazon presigned s3 url if you want to store the files on your own servers. Must set useCustomStorage to true or it will default API2PDF servers.

post.summary: Extract structured JSON from a PDF URL
post.description:
A JSON object as a payload is required within the body of the request. The following attributes of the JSON object are detailed below:

- `url` *(string, required)* - Url to the PDF file to parse.

- `fileName` *(string, optional)* - Specify a file name for the output JSON. Random name if not specified.

- `extraHTTPHeaders` *(dict, optional)* - A key-value object for specifying headers in a request to the URL.

- `storage` *(object, optional)* - set useCustomStorage to true and then provide a method and url, typically a PUT and an amazon presigned s3 url if you want to store the files on your own servers. Must set useCustomStorage to true or it will default API2PDF servers.

post.summary: Extract Markdown from a PDF URL
post.description:
A JSON object as a payload is required within the body of the request. The following attributes of the JSON object are detailed below:

- `url` *(string, required)* - Url to the PDF file to parse.

- `fileName` *(string, optional)* - Specify a file name for the output Markdown. Random name if not specified.

- `extraHTTPHeaders` *(dict, optional)* - A key-value object for specifying headers in a request to the URL.

- `storage` *(object, optional)* - set useCustomStorage to true and then provide a method and url, typically a PUT and an amazon presigned s3 url if you want to store the files on your own servers. Must set useCustomStorage to true or it will default API2PDF servers.

post.summary: Extract HTML from a PDF URL
post.description:
A JSON object as a payload is required within the body of the request. The following attributes of the JSON object are detailed below:

- `url` *(string, required)* - Url to the PDF file to parse.

- `fileName` *(string, optional)* - Specify a file name for the output HTML. Random name if not specified.

- `extraHTTPHeaders` *(dict, optional)* - A key-value object for specifying headers in a request to the URL.

- `storage` *(object, optional)* - set useCustomStorage to true and then provide a method and url, typically a PUT and an amazon presigned s3 url if you want to store the files on your own servers. Must set useCustomStorage to true or it will default API2PDF servers.

post.summary: Combine multiple PDF files into a single PDF file
post.description:
A JSON object as a payload is required within the body of the request. The following attributes of the JSON object are detailed below:

- `urls` *(list of strings, required)* - List of urls to the PDFs to merge

- `inline` *(boolean, optional)* - Open the PDF in a browser window. Default to true.

- `fileName` *(string, optional)* - Specify a file name for the output PDF. Random name if not specified.

- `extraHTTPHeaders` *(dict, optional)* - A key-value object for specifying headers in a request to the URL.

- `storage` *(object, optional)* - set useCustomStorage to true and then provide a method and url, typically a PUT and an amazon presigned s3 url if you want to store the files on your own servers. Must set useCustomStorage to true or it will default API2PDF servers.

post.summary: Add a password to an existing PDF
post.description:
A JSON object as a payload is required within the body of the request. The following attributes of the JSON object are detailed below:

- `url` *(string, required)* - Url to the PDF

- `userpassword` *(string, required)* - User password to set to lock down pdf

- `ownerpassword` *(string, optional)* - Owner password to set on pdf

- `inline` *(boolean, optional)* - Open the PDF in a browser window. Default to true.

- `fileName` *(string, optional)* - Specify a file name for the output PDF. Random name if not specified.

- `extraHTTPHeaders` *(dict, optional)* - A key-value object for specifying headers in a request to the URL.

- `storage` *(object, optional)* - set useCustomStorage to true and then provide a method and url, typically a PUT and an amazon presigned s3 url if you want to store the files on your own servers. Must set useCustomStorage to true or it will default API2PDF servers.

post.summary: Extract pages out of a PDF
post.description:
A JSON object as a payload is required within the body of the request. The following attributes of the JSON object are detailed below:

- `url` *(string, required)* - Url to the PDF

- `start` *(integer, optional)* - Start the range of the PDF to extract, indexed to 0, defaults to the first page of the PDF. Can be negative, meaning -1 would start as the last page of the PDF

- `end` *(integer, optional)* - End the range of the PDF to extract, indexed to 0, defaults to the last page of the PDF, can be negative.

- `inline` *(boolean, optional)* - Open the PDF in a browser window. Default to true.

- `fileName` *(string, optional)* - Specify a file name for the output PDF. Random name if not specified.

- `extraHTTPHeaders` *(dict, optional)* - A key-value object for specifying headers in a request to the URL.

- `storage` *(object, optional)* - set useCustomStorage to true and then provide a method and url, typically a PUT and an amazon presigned s3 url if you want to store the files on your own servers. Must set useCustomStorage to true or it will default API2PDF servers.

post.summary: Add a text watermark to every page of a PDF
post.description:
Overlay a text watermark (e.g. DRAFT, CONFIDENTIAL, DO NOT DISTRIBUTE) on every page of an existing PDF. The watermark is rendered with configurable font size, color, opacity, and rotation so it can serve as a diagonal stamp across each page.

- `url` *(string, required)* - URL to the source PDF file

- `text` *(string, optional)* - The watermark text to display. Defaults to "DRAFT"

- `fontSize` *(integer, optional)* - Font size in points. Defaults to 72

- `color` *(string, optional)* - Hex color code for the watermark text (e.g. "#FF0000" for red). Defaults to "#000000" (black)

- `opacity` *(number, optional)* - Opacity of the watermark from 0.0 (invisible) to 1.0 (fully opaque). Defaults to 0.15

- `rotation` *(integer, optional)* - Rotation angle in degrees. Negative values rotate counter-clockwise. Defaults to -45 (diagonal from bottom-left to top-right)

- `inline` *(boolean, optional)* - Open the PDF in a browser window. Defaults to true

- `fileName` *(string, optional)* - Specify a file name for the output PDF. Random name if not specified

- `extraHTTPHeaders` *(dict, optional)* - A key-value object for specifying headers in a request to the URL

- `storage` *(object, optional)* - Set useCustomStorage to true and then provide a method and url, typically a PUT and an Amazon presigned S3 url if you want to store the files on your own servers. Must set useCustomStorage to true or it will default API2PDF servers.

delete.description:
Delete a file on command instead of waiting 24 hours for self-delete. Make a DELETE call to /file/{responseId} where {responseId} is from the JSON object returned from the original call to generate the file.
      
### Authorize via Header of Request

**Authorization: YOUR-API-KEY**

get.summary: Check remaining credit balance on your account
get.description: Check remaining credit balance on your account

get.summary: Convert URL to PDF. Recommended to use Chrome endpoints instead.
get.description: Convert a URL or Web Page to PDF using wkhtmltopdf. This GET request is for convenience and does not support advanced options. Use the POST request for more flexibility.

post.summary: Convert URL to PDF. Recommended to use Chrome endpoints instead.
post.description:
A JSON object as a payload is required within the body of the request. The following attributes of the JSON object are detailed below:

- `url` *(string, required)* - Url to the web page to convert to PDF

- `inline` *(boolean, optional)* - Open the PDF in a browser window. Default to true.

- `fileName` *(string, optional)* - Specify a file name for the output PDF. Random name if not specified.

- `options` *(object, optional)* - Include advanced Wkhtmltopdf options like margins, headers, and footers. [See full list of advanced options here](https://www.api2pdf.com/documentation/advanced-options-wkhtmltopdf/).

- `enableToc` *(boolean, optional)* - Enable table of contents on your pdf by setting enableToc to true.

- `tocOptions` *(object, optional)* - set enableToc to true and then configure your ToC options add a Table of Contents to the top of your PDF automatically.

- `storage` *(object, optional)* - set useCustomStorage to true and then provide a method and url, typically a PUT and an amazon presigned s3 url if you want to store the files on your own servers. Must set useCustomStorage to true or it will default API2PDF servers.

post.summary: Convert raw HTML to PDF. Recommended to use Chrome endpoints instead.
post.description:
A JSON object as a payload is required within the body of the request. The following attributes of the JSON object are detailed below:

- `html` *(string, required)* - raw HTML to convert to PDF

- `inline` *(boolean, optional)* - Open the PDF in a browser window. Default to false.

- `fileName` *(string, optional)* - Specify a file name for the output PDF. Random name if not specified.

- `options` *(object, optional)* - Include advanced wkhtmltopdf options like margins, headers, and footers. [See full list of advanced options here](https://www.api2pdf.com/documentation/advanced-options-wkhtmltopdf/).

- `enableToc` *(boolean, optional)* - Enable table of contents on your pdf by setting enableToc to true.

- `tocOptions` *(object, optional)* - set enableToc to true and then configure your ToC options add a Table of Contents to the top of your PDF automatically.

- `storage` *(object, optional)* - set useCustomStorage to true and then provide a  method and url, typically a PUT and an amazon presigned s3 url if you want to store the files on your own servers. Must set useCustomStorage to true or it will default API2PDF servers.

get.summary: Generate bar codes and QR codes with ZXING.
get.description: `format` is most common CODE_39 or QR_CODE. See full list of options and documentation [here](https://www.api2pdf.com/documentation/advanced-options-zxing-zebra-crossing-barcodes/). Will default to image output, but can specify outputBinary=true to get a standard JSON response.

post.summary: Create a .zip file from multiple files
post.description: Pass in a list of urls to existing files along with desired file names and paths to subdirectories. This method will ingest those files and create a .zip and return the .zip in binary form in the response by default but you can specify outputBinary=false to get a standard JSON response. Be mindful of memory limits of the API.

ApiKeyInHeader.description: API Key needed to access the endpoints. Example: "Authorization: abc123"
ApiKeyInHeader.name: Authorization

ApiKeyInQuery.description: API Key needed to access the endpoints. Example: "?apiKey=abc123"
ApiKeyInQuery.name: apiKey

apiKey.name: Authorization
apiKey.description: API key for authentication. Get your key at portal.api2pdf.com

apiKeyQuery.name: apikey
apiKeyQuery.description: API key passed as query parameter
